name: Coinstar Kiosk
description_short: Coinstar operates self-service kiosks offering coin-to-cash conversion, cryptocurrency purchases, and cash transfer services at retail locations across the U.S.
description_long:
Coinstar is a widely-distributed kiosk operator that provides convenient financial services at retail locations, primarily grocery stores, across the United States. The company was recently acquired by Arctic Slope Regional Corporation (ASRC) as of February 2026. Coinstar's flagship service is Coins to Cash®, which allows customers to convert loose change into cash without counting or rolling coins, though fees vary by location.

Beyond coin conversion, Coinstar kiosks offer a diversified suite of financial services. Customers can exchange coins for eGift cards (with no-fee options available), donate coins to charities through the Coins that Count® program, purchase cryptocurrencies via Coinme or CINQ by Coinstar powered by Zero Hash, add cash to Amazon accounts, and transfer funds directly to checking accounts. The PayOMatic kiosk referenced operates in Long Island City, NY at 44-66 21st Street and notably does not accept bills for some services.

Coinstar distinguishes itself through multi-service kiosks that consolidate financial transactions traditionally requiring trips to banks or separate retailers. Their cryptocurrency integration (25+ crypto options through CINQ) and Amazon Cash partnership represent modern financial inclusion features. The availability of no-fee eGift card exchanges and charity donation options provide cost-conscious alternatives to standard cash conversion.

The main limitation is that service availability varies significantly by location—not all kiosks offer all services, and the specific PayOMatic location referenced does not accept bills. Fees for coin-to-cash conversion and account transfers vary by location and financial institution. While convenient for retail-location access, customers should verify services at their specific kiosk before visiting.
pros: No-fee eGift card exchange options available at select kiosks
Convenient retail locations (grocery stores) with multiple services in one kiosk
Coins to Cash® service eliminates need for customers to count, sort, or roll coins
Integrated cryptocurrency purchasing through Coinme and CINQ by Coinstar
Direct account transfer capability to skip bank lines
Charity donation option (Coins that Count®) for socially-conscious users
Amazon Cash integration for shoppers without credit/debit cards
cons: Coin-to-cash conversion fees vary by location, reducing actual cash payout
Service availability highly variable—not all kiosks offer all services listed
Specific kiosk locations (like PayOMatic) do not accept bills for cash services
Account transfer fees vary by financial institution partner
Requires app download for cryptocurrency and Amazon Cash services
best_for: Consumers with significant loose change seeking quick, convenient conversion
Retail shoppers without credit/debit cards interested in Amazon Cash
Cryptocurrency-curious users wanting easy entry point with cash
Charity-minded individuals looking to donate spare change
diagnosis: Coinstar kiosks are best for consumers seeking convenient, retail-accessible financial services—particularly those with loose change, interest in cryptocurrency, or alternative payment methods. The critical caveat is that service availability, fees, and accepted payment types vary significantly by specific kiosk location, requiring verification before visiting.
services: Coins to Cash® conversion (fees vary by location)
eGift card exchange with NO FEE options
CINQ by Coinstar cryptocurrency wallet and exchange
CINQ by Coinstar debit card services
Kiosk locator and service filtering tools
